mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and a decline in real consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized pullback in household spending and investment, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 2% and RSAFS falls > 1% in same month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical co-contraction episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects supply-side resilience that eases input cost pressures, allowing profit margins to expand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO rises during high CPI periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation falls below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates anchor to the higher policy floor.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening economic momentum, which boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy drops below 50% over any 12-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between payroll growth months and contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and a rise in residential housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 40% of 60-day windows, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant when controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a sharp rebound in industrial production relative to lagging consumer credit indicates supply chain normalization outpacing demand recovery, which compresses input costs and supports equity margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% while RSAFS growth decelerates
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO outpaces RSAFS growth, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of qualifying episodes.
